page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 CPU model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1895.0 and a strong bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 14.48 GB/sec. The instance achieves a top-tier PassMark memory score of 3199.23, supported by a cached read-only memory bandwidth of 925.09 GB/sec. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff, as evidenced by a weak PassMark CPU encryption test score of 1504.12. Large-model LLM tasks also show limitations, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model registering a poor speed of 15.67 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model remains average at 11.77 tokens/sec.

The m8gn.2xlarge is network and EBS optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides a high-density resource profile with competitive memory throughput per unit of compute, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 architectures for balanced workloads. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and lightweight text processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 32.0 GB of system memory, a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1895.0 and a top-tier PassMark memory score of 3199.23. However, the instance exhibits tradeoffs in cryptographic tasks, scoring weakly on the PassMark CPU encryption test, and shows poor prompt processing speeds for medium-sized LLM models. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high memory bandwidth, the m8gn.2xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for ARM-native deployments. It is suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB of memory, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 8 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high memory bandwidth, and optimized network performance for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier memory performance with a PassMark memory score of 3199.23
Tradeoffs in cryptographic operations and medium-model LLM prompt processing
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
